# UC-11.6.1 · Proofpoint TAP URL Defense Click-Time Protection Follow-Through Rate

## Description

URL rewriting is only effective if users stop after the warning. Measuring how often rewritten malicious links still record a risky follow-through highlights training gaps, aggressive users, or mobile clients skipping interstitials.

## Value

Quantifies the human layer of TAP and drives targeted simulations or policy tightening for repeat click-through populations.

## Implementation

(1) Ingest Proofpoint click telemetry with rewrite and final disposition fields. (2) Align field names to your TA version; use `spath` if JSON is nested. (3) Exclude mailer daemons via lookup. (4) Baseline departments. (5) Pair with LMS or HR nudges for outliers.

## SPL

```spl
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint:click" earliest=-7d
| eval rewritten=coalesce(urlRewritten, URLRewritten, rewritten, "unknown")
| eval disposition=coalesce(threatStatus, ThreatStatus, disposition, "")
| eval user=coalesce(clickUser, recipient, Recipient, "")
| where rewritten="true" OR match(lower(_raw), "(?i)rewrite")
| stats count(eval(match(lower(disposition),"(?i)malicious|blocked|click.*block"))) as blocked_clicks count as total_clicks by user
| eval follow_through_pct=round(100*(total_clicks-blocked_clicks)/total_clicks,2)
| where total_clicks>=5 AND follow_through_pct>20
| sort -follow_through_pct
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (follow-through % by user or OU), line chart (weekly rate), table of top users.

## References

- [Proofpoint — Targeted Attack Protection (URL defense)](https://www.proofpoint.com/us/products/advanced-threat-protection/targeted-attack-protection)
